item.title: Iran War Live Updates: U.S. Rescues Officer From Downed Fighter Jet in Iran, Trump Says
item.description: The rescue followed a two-day race between U.S. and Iranian forces to reach the injured airman, officials said. On Saturday, Israel attacked Iran’s largest petrochemical industrial complex.

item.title: Keir Starmer 'concerned' over Kanye West UK festival dates
item.description: The rapper has drawn significant criticism over his antisemitic and offensive remarks in recent years.

item.title: What we know so far about rescue of US airman in Iran
item.description: The US has rescued the missing crew member of the US F-15 fighter jet which was shot down over southern Iran.

item.title: Here’s the latest.
US rescues second fighter jet airman shot down over Iran
item.description: Trump hails recovery of weapons system officer who spent more than 24 hours on the ground

item.title: China Built the World’s Drone Industry. Now It’s Locking Down the Skies.
item.description: The Chinese government tightened rules to curb what it described as illegal drone use, but some users say the changes are now restricting too many flights.

item.title: Stagflation fears demolish confidence in UK housing
item.description: Shares across the sector are taking a beating

item.title: How we gave up on forgiveness
item.description: What was once seen as a virtue is now viewed as a moral weakness — leaving a society mired in toxicity

item.title: Military briefing: How Iran keeps firing missiles under bombardment
item.description: Tehran is still launching retaliatory strikes on Israel and Gulf states

item.title: 400% gains for AI stocks help drive Hong Kong IPOs to 5-year high
item.description: Deal backlogs and stricter quality controls are pushing some tech firms back to mainland Chinese listings

item.title: Pharma just ‘scratching the surface’ of weight-loss drug market
item.description: Novo Nordisk chief estimates industry reaching only 15% of potential customers at most

item.title: UK courts Anthropic to expand in London after US defence clash
item.description: Keir Starmer’s government steps up efforts to get American AI start-up to grow its presence in Britain

item.title: Zimbabwe’s ‘Crocodile’ leader eyes role as president for life
item.description: Emmerson Mnangagwa plans to amend constitution to extend tenure to 2030 — and possibly beyond

item.title: Campaign to curb cars in Berlin sparks uproar ahead of election
item.description: Conservatives and far right are opposing a citizens’ initiative to restrict traffic in the German capital

item.title: Renters’ Rights Act brings big changes to UK property market
item.description: New rules aim to provide safety and security for tenants, but landlords are anxious

item.title: US payments group with $20bn market value aims to boost UK truck electrification
item.description: Corpay is partnering with Voltempo in building UK’s largest charging network for electric lorries

item.title: ‘Police were never designed for this’: Pioneering UK force rethinks approach to domestic abuse
item.description: Avon and Somerset to apply successful rape investigation techniques to broader field of offences

item.title: Countries must not hoard fuel during Iran war, warns IEA
item.description: Fatih Birol makes veiled reference to China as he urges nations to avoid export bans despite worsening supply shock

item.title: Five elections, one month: India’s Modi faces voters amid Iran war risks
item.description: PM accused of flat-footed response as conflict in Middle East puts economy under strain

item.title: Here’s What Happened in the War in the Middle East on Saturday
item.description: The U.S. military’s search-and-rescue operation for a missing American airman entered its second day. The Israeli military struck a major petrochemical complex in Iran.

item.title: Israel Strikes Iran’s Largest Petrochemical Complex
item.description: The sprawling complex in the city of Mahshahr was taken offline after strikes hit two utility plants that provided basic services like gas and power, Iranian officials said.

item.title: Dozens Killed in Haiti Massacre as International Force Trickles In
item.description: Gangs tore through several rural communities last weekend, underscoring the challenges that will face the new, U.N.-backed Gang Suppression Force starting to enter the country.

item.title: Comedians tell ministers lack of funding is no laughing matter
item.description: Culture Minister Ian Murray has agreed to work with the comedy industry to boost support after talks.

item.title: Trump Wants to Make Deportation Deals. Autocrats Are Ready to Listen.
item.description: The White House has turned deportations, a signature domestic issue, into a major piece of foreign policy. Here’s what we know about the program.

item.title: Deadly Earthquake and Floods Worsen Afghanistan’s Troubles
item.description: Floods have killed at least 77 people this week and destroyed or damaged thousands of homes, and an earthquake on Friday killed a dozen more.

item.title: Satellite image company restricts Mideast content, citing a U.S. government request.
US says it has arrested relatives of late Iranian ​general Qasem ​Soleimani
item.description: The niece and grand-niece of Qasem Soleimani are in the custody of US Immigration and Customs Enforcement, officials said.

item.title: German males under 45 may need military approval for long stays abroad
item.description: Under the law, travel approvals must generally be granted and it remains unclear how the rule would be enforced if breached.

item.title: Trump threatens Iran with ‘hell’ as search continues for US airman
item.description: President says ‘time is running out’ to reopen Strait of Hormuz

item.title: Search for missing airman presents serious test for US
item.description: The stakes could rise further if the weapons system officer of the downed F15 Eagle plane is captured by Iran.

item.title: Here’s how two airmen would have ejected from the F-15E.
item.description: A step-by-step look at the process by which the two crew members probably ejected from the fighter jet.

item.title: Russian attack on Ukraine market kills five
item.description: A Russian drone hit a busy spot in the southern Ukrainian town on Saturday morning, injuring another 21 people.

item.title: World's oldest leader to get a deputy for first time in 43-year rule
item.description: Cameroon's President Paul Biya, 93, won his eighth successive term last year in a disputed election.

item.title: U.S. Fighter Jet Downed Over Iran Was Probably Based at R.A.F. Lakenheath, U.K. Airfield
item.description: A military analyst identified markings consistent with a squadron based at R.A.F. Lakenheath, one of two British bases that host the largest U.S. fighter jet operation in Europe.

item.title: Three Charged With Arson in Attack on U.K. Jewish Charity’s Ambulances
item.description: A fourth suspect was arrested after showing up at a hearing for the defendants, the police said. The March attack was widely condemned but has not been declared a terrorist incident.

item.title: Stephen Lewis Was a Singular Man on a Mission
item.description: Heartbreaking scenes of death from H.I.V. in Africa enraged and fueled the work of the Canadian AIDS activist and politician who died on Tuesday.

item.title: The shadowy group claiming attacks around Europe
item.description: Ashab al-Yamin, which uses Iran-linked Telegram channels, has taken responsibility for assaults on ambulances, a synagogue and banks

item.title: What We Know About the F-15E Strike Eagle Shot Down by Iran
item.description: An F-15E Strike Eagle was shot down over Iran and an A-10 Warthog crashed near the Strait of Hormuz, officials said on Friday. Two airmen were rescued, and one is unaccounted for.

item.title: A projectile hit near Iran’s Bushehr nuclear plant, killing a worker.
item.description: There was no reported increase in radiation levels. A string of recent attacks near the Persian Gulf facility has fueled fears of a nuclear accident.

item.title: US searches for crew member after fighter jet shot down over Iran
item.description: One of the two personnel on the American F-15E aircraft has been rescued while a second warplane has been lost

item.title: Senegal bans ministers from foreign travel as oil price rise bites
item.description: The fuel cost is nearly double what the government budgeted for putting pressure on stretched finances.

item.title: In Hungary’s election it is war versus wallets
item.description: Péter Magyar’s focus on pocket book issues may give him the edge over Orbán

item.title: Iranians Seek Respite in Nature Amid the War
item.description: Families gathered for picnics and games this week to mark the end of the Iranian new year holiday, seizing on a brief chance to celebrate.

item.title: Europe’s Options in the Strait of Hormuz: Few, and Risky
item.description: European leaders and other officials have ideas for bringing shipping back to the strait once the Iran war ends. But none of them are sure bets.

item.title: Russia chose 'Easter escalation' over ceasefire, says Zelensky
item.description: Six civilians were killed and 40 others injured as Russia launched hundreds of drones and missiles.

item.title: Football rally in Peru leaves one dead and dozens injured
item.description: Hundreds of Alianza Lima fans had gathered for a pregame rally ahead of a match against local rivals Universitario de Deportes.

item.title: 'Not fit for purpose' - the secret history of a deadly phrase
item.description: The four word phrase - first uttered by Labour Home Secretary John Reid - has become a by-word for incompetence.

item.title: Epstein Presented Himself to Indian Tycoon as a Trump White House Insider
item.description: The convicted sex offender gave Anil Ambani information on appointments and foreign policy. Some seemed prescient, though there was no evidence he was close to the administration.

item.title: Japan cracks down on its wayward cyclists
item.description: Enforcement regime will levy fines for infractions including using headphones and riding with one hand

item.title: Go to court and lose out on £9bn car finance redress scheme, says FCA boss
item.description: Comments by Nikhil Rathi position watchdog for stand-off with claims management companies and law firms

item.title: The Art of No Deal: Trump’s Approach to the Iran War
item.description: “The plan is not to have a plan,” the staff writer Susan B. Glasser says.

item.title: 'We want a voice in our land' - the people evicted to build Nigeria's capital
item.description: People evicted to build Nigeria's capital accuse the government of failing to fulfill its promises to them.

item.title: How Bombay went from a fort city to a bustling metropolis
item.description: A new exhibition charts Bombay's passage through the centuries via paintings, photographs and prints.

item.title: Faced with new energy shock, Europe asks if reviving nuclear is the answer
item.description: As war drives up gas and fuel prices, Europeans turn again to the issue of energy independence.

item.title: After 16 years in power, could Viktor Orban finally be unseated?
item.description: Hungary is going to the polls in nine days - after 16 years in power, can Viktor Orban be unseated?

item.title: Ex-US Army Pilot Recalls Battle for Survival When Shot Down in Iraq
item.description: Ronald Young Jr. was in the Army when his Apache Longbow copter went down during the U.S. invasion of Iraq.

item.title: Cuba begins releasing more than 2,000 prisoners as US pressure mounts
item.description: US President Trump has made clear his desire to change Cuba's leadership and has placed the country under an oil embargo.

item.title: A captive American in Iran could lead to further escalation
item.description: It could prompt Donald Trump to follow through on his worst threats

item.title: US warns its citizens to leave Lebanon citing escalating security risks
item.description: The advisory follows recent military developments, including a US jet reportedly being shot down and Israeli strikes on parts of Beirut.

item.title: Unanswered questions remain after Australia's most wanted fugitive killed in standoff
item.description: Double-murderer Dezi Freeman evaded capture for seven months in the bush but police believe he had help.

item.title: Marmalades may need to be relabelled under post-Brexit food deal
item.description: The breakfast favourite will be legally renamed when Britain aligns with new EU labelling rules.

item.title: Could There Be a Popular Uprising in Cuba?
item.description: There is no organized opposition on the island because many critics of the regime have fled. But anti-government protests have been growing.

item.title: French-owned ship passes through Strait of Hormuz
item.description: It appears to be the first ship owned by a major European firm to go through the strait since the conflict began.

item.title: US deports eight people 'of African origin' to Uganda
item.description: The Uganda Law Society criticises the deportation, saying it was illegal and "dehumanising".

item.title: Italy's Uffizi Galleries targeted in cyber-attack but deny security breach
item.description: Hackers were reported to have infiltrated IT systems - but the gallery says its works are safe.

item.title: Experts dispute US account of deadly Iran sports hall strike in Lamerd
item.description: Six weapons experts have contested the US claim that video evidence suggests an Iranian missile could have hit the hall.

item.title: Italy and Gattuso 'mutually terminate' contract
item.description: Manager Gennaro Gattuso and Italy agree to "mutually terminate" his contract after their failure to qualify for a third successive World Cup.

item.title: France's Muslim gathering ban overturned by courts
item.description: The Paris police department had argued that the four-day gathering was a security threat because it could be a target of terrorism.

item.title: International law experts allege violations in Iran war
item.description: The White House says it is making the entire region safer by eliminating short and long-term threats.

item.title: When will the Iran war end? Tracing the Trump administration's timelines
item.description: Experts say many US presidents have offered a timeline for a conflict - only to then shift their estimates.

item.title: Burkina Faso must 'forget' about democracy, military leader says
item.description: Capt Ibrahim Traoré, who seized power in 2023, says democratic rule would not work for his country.

item.title: 'This has got me worried': Iranians fear what comes next after US strike on Karaj bridge
item.description: President Trump has warned Iran of strikes on bridges and electric power plants if its leaders do not agree to his terms to end the war.

item.title: The 'Swiss Leicester' closing on their first title
item.description: A decade after Leicester unexpectedly won the Premier League, FC Thun of Switzerland are closing on one of football's greatest underdog triumphs.

item.title: Myanmar's coup leader who set off a brutal civil war becomes president
item.description: General Min Aung Hlaing has been chosen as the next president by the newly-elected parliament.

item.title: Watch: Who is the coup leader who will be Myanmar's next president?
item.description: Min Aung Hlaing presided over his last military parade in Myanmar before he takes up the role of president.

item.title: Police offered support to tackle rising threats against MPs
item.description: Reports of crimes against MPs have more than doubled since 2019, reaching almost 1,000 last year.

item.title: Fears cost of water and beer to soar as India's scorching summer hits
item.description: Makers of plastic and glass bottles are struggling to access raw materials as the war squeezes supplies.

item.title: Four toddlers stabbed to death at Ugandan nursery school
item.description: The suspect is in custody but his motive is unknown, the police say in a brief statement.

item.title: Sixteen Kenyans missing in Russia after army recruitment
item.description: More than 250 Kenyans have gone to fight for Russia in Ukraine, mostly willingly, Kenya's foreign minister says.

item.title: Ros Atkins on…the cost of the Iran war
item.description: The BBC's Analysis Editor Ros Atkins takes a closer look at US costs as it continues its war with with Iran.

item.title: Man dies in storm near Athens as Saharan dust shrouds Crete
item.description: Several regions are flooded in mainland Greece, while a Saharan dust storm envelopes the island of Crete.

item.title: UK and allies discuss sanctions to stop Iran blocking Strait of Hormuz
item.description: Yvette Cooper said coordinated diplomatic and economic measures are required to reopen the shipping lane.

item.title: Iran's two largest steel plants shut down due to air strikes, companies say
item.description: The strikes, which Iran says were first launched by Israel in co-ordination with the US last week, could cause major damage to the Iranian economy.

item.title: Rising costs are changing shopping habits in one of Wales' poorest areas
item.description: Shoppers are paying in cash to keep finances in check, not buying in bulk, and investing in higher-value items.

item.title: 'I haven't slept for days': Iranians describe mounting desperation after a month of war
item.description: Ordinary people from various parts of Iran describe expanding strikes, economic pain and fear of repression.

item.title: Iranian Nobel laureate suffered suspected heart attack in prison, family says
item.description: Narges Mohammadi's brother says he fears her life is in imminent danger and she needs to be transferred to a hospital.

item.title: Davey calls for emergency cuts to fuel duty and rail fares
item.description: The Liberal Democrat leader says his transport support package would ease the impacts of the war in Iran.

item.title: Lecturer who called Israel a terrorist state to remain Plaid Cymru candidate
item.description: Plaid Cymru says that a university lecturer who called Israel a "terrorist state" will remain a candidate.

item.title: Ancient golden helmet recovered more than a year after Dutch heist
item.description: The helmet and three golden bracelets were stolen in January 2025, but one bracelet is yet to be found.

item.title: Iran's focus on survival means same regime still firmly in place
item.description: Tehran is showing resilience despite Trump's claims of regime change, writes BBC Persian editor Amir Azimi.

item.title: 'Be serious... don't speak every day': Macron criticises Trump approach to Iran war
item.description: The comments were an apparent jab at the US leader's sometimes contradictory stance on the US-Israeli war against Iran.

item.title: Swinney softens stance on North Sea drilling as oil prices soar
item.description: The first minister said energy security should be a primary concern when making decisions on oil and gas projects.

item.title: Donald Trump’s Case for War Fails to Mention How to Win It
item.description: The President poses an existential question: Can everything be going according to the plan with Iran if there is no plan?

item.title: Iconic 19th Century painting sets Indian art record with $17.9m sale
item.description: Yashoda and Krishna, painted by Raja Ravi Varma, beat a record set by an MF Husain work last year.

item.title: US lifts sanctions on Venezuelan interim leader Delcy Rodríguez
item.description: It is the latest sign of warming relations between the US and Venezuela after Nicolás Maduro was seized in a commando raid.

item.title: Reform UK pledges to cut welfare to keep pensions triple lock
item.description: Leader Nigel Farage said the party had reached a "settled position" on the triple lock after debating scrapping it.

item.title: Trump says Iran war objectives 'nearing completion' in address to nation
item.description: The US president's first televised address of the conflict with Iran did little to quell uneasy markets.

item.title: Reform housing spokesman sacked over Grenfell remarks
item.description: A group representing the bereaved of Grenfell call Simon Dudley's comments "ignorant and callous".

item.title: New Brazil law allows separated couples joint custody over pets
item.description: Lawmakers in the Brazilian Congress view the law change as a reflection of pets' importance for their owners.

item.title: New laws to make it easier to cancel subscriptions and get refunds
item.description: Consumers will be able to cancel unwanted subscriptions 'at the click of a button', the government said.
